metadata.summary: Make JavaScript apps indexable. Every route must deliver title, description, canonical, og:image, JSON-LD in server-rendered HTML, not post-hydration DOM. Verify with curl.

content.title: SEO for SPAs
content.summary: A methodology for making JavaScript-heavy applications indexable. Every public route must deliver title, meta[name=description], link[rel=canonical], og:image, and valid JSON-LD in the server-rendered HTML — not just the post-hydration DOM. Verify with curl -A "Googlebot/2.1", not Playwright. Treat SEO as a build-time contract verified by Lighthouse CI on every PR.

why.p: Googlebot renders JS but Bing, Yandex, and social crawlers often do not. SSR/SSG/ISR ensures the first HTML response carries discoverable content. Dynamic meta tags drive social sharing (Open Graph, Twitter Card). JSON-LD structured data enables rich results. Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, INP) directly affect search ranking. Without CI verification, meta tags silently regress across route changes.

list.item: Migrating CSR React/Vue/Angular app to SSR/SSG/ISR for indexable pages
Auditing meta tags and OG/Twitter cards across dynamic routes
Generating sitemap.xml + robots.txt from CMS or DB content at build time
Wiring JSON-LD structured data (Product, Article, Breadcrumb, Organization)
Fixing Core Web Vitals regressions surfaced by PageSpeed Insights
Building OG image generation routes for share previews
Authenticated dashboards or SaaS app shells — no SEO value, ship as CSR
Internal tools or extranets behind login walls
Sites where social share previews and search ranking are not goals

section.name: ssr-requirement
section.p: SSR Requirement: every public route must deliver meta tags in server-rendered HTML.

rule.statement: Every public route must deliver all required meta tags in the server-rendered HTML, not only after client-side hydration. Verify with curl -A "Googlebot/2.1", not Playwright or browser DevTools.
rule.rationale: Googlebot renders JS but Bing, Yandex, and all social crawlers often do not. Post-hydration DOM is invisible to them. curl fetches the raw SSR output, which is ground truth for indexability.

section.name: required-meta-fields
section.p: Required Meta Fields Per Route: title, description, canonical, OG and Twitter tags.

rule.statement: Every public page must include: title (30-65 chars), meta[name=description] (120-160 chars), link[rel=canonical] with absolute URL hardcoded from env (never window.location.host), og:title, og:description, og:image (1200x630), og:url, og:type, twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image.
rule.rationale: Missing any of these causes partial indexing, broken social shares, or reduced click-through rate. The canonical URL from window.location.host leaks preview-deploy URLs as canonical, causing duplicate-content penalties.

example.note: NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL is set to the canonical production domain. Preview deploys use a different env value.

section.name: canonical-url-rule
section.p: Canonical URL Rule: hardcode the canonical origin in environment variable.

rule.statement: Hardcode the canonical origin in an environment variable (SITE_URL). Never derive it from the incoming request host. Preview deployments must have a different SITE_URL or suppress canonical tags entirely.
rule.rationale: Preview deploy URLs (deploy-preview-123.netlify.app) indexed as canonical URLs cause duplicate-content penalties that suppress the production URL in search results.

section.name: link-rules-for-crawlability
section.p: Link Rules for Crawlability: anchor tags with href attributes.

rule.statement: All navigation links must be anchor tags with href attributes. Never use onClick + router.push() without an accompanying href. Crawler bots do not execute JavaScript click handlers.
rule.rationale: Links without href are invisible to crawlers. Internal pages reachable only via JS navigation are not indexed regardless of SSR quality.

section.name: json-ld-structured-data
section.p: JSON-LD Structured Data: schema.org types and validation.

rule.statement: Inject JSON-LD structured data as a script[type=application/ld+json] tag in the server-rendered HTML. Use schema.org types: Product, Article, BreadcrumbList, Organization. Validate against Google Rich Results Test in CI. Do not include undefined values — JSON.stringify drops them but typed nesting can still produce invalid output.
rule.rationale: Rich snippets (star ratings, prices, breadcrumbs in search results) increase click-through rates by 20-30%. Invalid JSON-LD silently produces no rich result with no error surfaced in the browser.

rule.statement: When injecting JSON-LD via dangerouslySetInnerHTML, escape </script> inside the JSON string: JSON.stringify(data).replace(/</g, '\\u003c'). Otherwise a string value containing "</script>" breaks the page.
rule.rationale: An unescaped </script> inside a JSON string closes the script tag early, causing a parse error that breaks the entire page for crawlers and users.

section.name: sitemap-generation
section.p: Sitemap Generation: build-time generation with content-driven lastmod.

rule.statement: Generate sitemap.xml at build time from CMS or DB content. Set lastmod from the content's updatedAt field, not build time. Split sitemaps at 50k URLs or 50MB and emit a sitemap index. Submit to Google Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools via their APIs.
rule.rationale: Build-time lastmod ("everything changed today") causes crawlers to re-crawl unchanged pages, wasting crawl budget. Content-driven lastmod signals only genuinely changed pages.

rule.statement: robots.txt must disallow /api/, admin pages, and search result pages with infinite facet combinations. Allow everything else. Reference the sitemap URL.
rule.rationale: Faceted search URLs (/products?color=red&size=M&brand=X) create infinite near-duplicate pages that exhaust crawl budget without adding ranking value.

section.name: isr-and-on-demand-revalidation
section.p: ISR and On-Demand Revalidation: webhook-driven freshness for editorial sites.

rule.statement: For ISR pages, call the on-demand revalidation webhook after any CMS content edit. Do not rely solely on the revalidate interval for editorial sites. Set the interval as a safety net only.
rule.rationale: ISR revalidate windows mean fresh content can sit behind stale meta for hours or days. Editors publishing urgent corrections see old titles/descriptions in search results until the interval expires.

section.name: core-web-vitals
section.p: Core Web Vitals: LCP image priority and Lighthouse CI.

rule.statement: Set next/image priority only on the LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) image. Applying priority broadly triggers preload spam that degrades performance scores. Run Lighthouse CI on every PR and block merges on score regression.
rule.rationale: LCP directly affects search ranking. Preload spam on non-LCP images delays the actual LCP resource, worsening the score.

antipattern.description: Rendering critical SEO content (title, description, product name, article body) only on the client after hydration.
antipattern.reason: Social crawlers and many search engine bots do not execute JavaScript. Client-only content is invisible to them. The page appears blank or generic in search results and social shares.

antipattern.description: Returning HTTP 200 with an empty or "not found" UI for missing slugs instead of HTTP 404.
antipattern.reason: Search Console treats 200+empty as a soft 404 and flags it. Crawl budget is wasted on these URLs. Use notFound: true in getStaticProps or return 404 explicitly.

section.name: playwright-for-verification
section.p: Using Playwright for SSR Verification: wrong tool for crawl verification.

antipattern.description: Verifying meta tags by reading the post-hydration DOM via Playwright instead of fetching raw SSR HTML with curl.
antipattern.reason: Playwright returns the fully hydrated browser DOM, which includes client-side meta injected after JS executes. curl -A "Googlebot/2.1" fetches what the crawler actually receives.

section.name: window-location-as-canonical
section.p: Window Location as Canonical Source: deploy previews as canonical.

antipattern.description: Deriving the canonical URL from window.location.hostname or request.headers.host at runtime.
antipattern.reason: Preview deployment domains (deploy-preview-123.netlify.app) become canonical URLs, suppressing the production URL in search results.

section.name: og-image-escaping
section.p: og:image Wrong Meta Property: incorrect Open Graph schema.

antipattern.description: Using product:price:amount instead of og:price:amount (or vice versa) for product Open Graph meta tags.
antipattern.reason: Facebook product schema uses product:price:amount. og:price:amount is not a standard property. Wrong properties silently produce no rich preview. Cross-check against ogp.me before shipping.

section.name: sitemap-lastmod
section.p: Sitemap lastmod Set to Build Time: wrong freshness signal.

antipattern.description: Setting lastmod in sitemap.xml to the build timestamp rather than the content's actual updatedAt date.
antipattern.reason: Googlebot sees that every URL changed on every deploy and re-crawls the entire site, wasting crawl budget on unchanged pages and delaying indexing of actually updated content.

template.purpose: React SEO component (Next.js Head + react-helmet-async) with all required fields
Build-time bash script that curl-fetches each route and asserts meta presence
